# Semantic HTML Tags Reference

Below is a comprehensive list of semantic HTML tags categorized by their function.

---

## 1. Document Structure & Layout (Macro-Semantics)

These tags define the major regions and sections of a webpage.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<header>` | Introductory content, mastheads, or top-level navigation for a page or section. |
| `<nav>` | A block containing major navigation links. |
| `<main>` | The unique, primary content of the document. |
| `<section>` | A thematic grouping of related content, typically with a heading. |
| `<article>` | Independent, self-contained content that could be reused elsewhere (e.g., blog posts, news stories). |
| `<aside>` | Content that is indirectly related to the main content, such as sidebars or callouts. |
| `<footer>` | The footer for a document or section, often containing contact info, site maps, or copyright data. |
| `<address>` | Contact information for the author or owner of a document. |

---

## 2. Text Content & Headings

These tags provide meaning to specific fragments of text.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<h1>`–`<h6>` | Section headings in order of hierarchical importance. |
| `<p>` | A standalone paragraph of text. |
| `<blockquote>` | A long quotation from another source. |
| `<cite>` | A reference to a creative work (e.g., a book or song title). |
| `<hr>` | A thematic break between paragraph-level elements. |
| `<pre>` | Preformatted text where whitespace is preserved. |

---

## 3. Inline Text Semantics

These tags define the meaning or importance of words within a block of text.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<a>` | A hyperlink to another resource. |
| `<strong>` | Text of strong importance or urgency. |
| `<em>` | Text with stressed emphasis. |
| `<mark>` | Text that is highlighted or relevant for reference. |
| `<time>` | A specific period or date/time. |
| `<abbr>` | An abbreviation or acronym. |
| `<code>` | A fragment of computer code. |
| `<dfn>` | The defining instance of a term. |
| `<kbd>` | User input, typically from a keyboard. |
| `<samp>` | Sample output from a computer program. |
| `<var>` | A variable in a mathematical expression or programming context. |
| `<small>` | Side comments or small print (like legal disclaimers). |
| `<sub>` / `<sup>` | Subscript and superscript text. |
| `<ins>` / `<del>` | Inserted and deleted text to show document edits. |

---

## 4. Interactive & Disclosure Elements

These tags define elements with built-in interactive behaviors.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<details>` | A disclosure widget from which a user can obtain additional information. |
| `<summary>` | A visible heading or label for a `<details>` element. |
| `<dialog>` | A dialog box or modal window. |
| `<button>` | A clickable button for triggering actions. |

---

## 5. Media & Multimedia

These tags provide semantic context for images, video, and audio.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<figure>` | Self-contained content, like an illustration or diagram. |
| `<figcaption>` | A caption for the content within a `<figure>`. |
| `<picture>` | A container used to specify multiple image resources. |
| `<audio>` / `<video>` | Used to embed sound or movie content. |

---

## 6. Lists & Tables

These tags represent structured data or grouped items.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<ul>` / `<ol>` / `<li>` | Unordered and ordered lists with individual list items. |
| `<dl>` / `<dt>` / `<dd>` | Description lists containing terms and their definitions. |
| `<table>` | Data organized in rows and columns. |
| `<caption>` | A title or explanation for a table. |
| `<thead>` / `<tbody>` / `<tfoot>` | Organizational groups for table rows. |
| `<th>` / `<tr>` / `<td>` | Table headers, rows, and data cells. |

---

## 7. Form Elements

While all form tags have functionality, these specifically describe the purpose of the data collection.

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `<form>` | A section for user input. |
| `<label>` | A caption for a specific form control. |
| `<fieldset>` | A group of related controls within a form. |
| `<legend>` | A caption for a `<fieldset>`. |
| `<output>` | The result of a calculation or user action. |
| `<meter>` / `<progress>` | Scalar measurements or task progress bars. |
